items.title: 'Never trust a label': Mystery violin willed to youth orchestra

children.content: A violin gifted to not-for-profit organisation Music for Canberra bears the hallmarks of early 18th century European style. But one expert says only forensic testing can prove its true origins.

gallery.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

items.title: Fears $1m to remove lead-contaminated soil in Captains Flat 'won't go very far'

children.content: Residents of Captains Flat have welcomed the promise of a $1 million fund to help them remediate lead contamination on private properties, but some fear it will not be enough.

image.altText: Signs warning of dangerous lead contamination at the road to a closed mine.

items.title: Australian in Israeli Defence Force committed war crimes, report alleges

children.content: The Australian Federal Police is considering a brief of evidence from the Australian Centre for International Justice, detailing alleged war crimes in Gaza by an Australian citizen who was in the Israeli Defence Force.

image.altText: Badge on the sleeve of Australian Federal Police officer's uniform.

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

gallery.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

thumbnail.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Jo Clay elected as leader of ACT Greens, Andrew Braddock named deputy

children.content: Jo Clay is elected leader of the ACT Greens in a party vote, with Andrew Braddock as deputy leader, ushering in a new era after Shane Rattenbury's 14 years at the helm.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair and a green blouse speaks, standing outside near a green hedge.

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: Canberra water catchment shows signs of climate change

children.content: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

gallery.altText: Views of a river in a national park

thumbnail.altText: Views of a river in a national park

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia’s bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

articles.title: Surveillance increased on reef island after bird flu arrival

children.content: A vulnerable colony of red-tailed tropicbirds is being monitored for the deadly H5 bird flu, with fears the impact on Australian native bird populations could be significant.

image.altText: A fluffy white bird with a black beak

articles.title: First case of deadly H5N1 bird flu confirmed in NSW

children.content: The first case of the deadly H5 bird flu is confirmed in a giant petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast.

image.altText: Bennetts Beach NSW Mid North Coast

gallery.altText: Black swans at swan river in Perth.

thumbnail.altText: Black swans at swan river in Perth.

items.title: Investors consider class action over super scam

children.content: Investors who lost out in a superannuation scam are considering a potential class action.

image.altText: A middle-aged woman standing in front of a caravan

items.title: Socceroos match shows where Labor's gambling ad laws fall short

children.content: There should be rules against jumping on the World Cup bandwagon to illustrate a political point, but the Socceroos' valiant effort against Egypt offers a timely real-world test.

image.altText: Socceroos fans look sad

items.title: For 60 years, Play School has never broken this rule

children.content: Australian Story is given rare behind-the-scenes access to Play School as it celebrates 60 years on air. An intimate look at the creative process of the iconic children's TV show that's still guided by one golden rule.

gallery.altText: A trio of three people standing on the set of the children's show Play School. Woman holding Little Ted Toy, another hold Humpty

thumbnail.altText: A trio of three people standing on the set of the children's show Play School. Woman holding Little Ted Toy, another hold Humpty

items.title: Keeper hurls ball at French star after ignored handshake in testy clash

children.content: Kylian Mbappé ignores a handshake request from the Paraguay goalkeeper after a World Cup clash that ended with the South American team being labelled as a 'disgrace'.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappé gestures behind him

items.title: France finally breaks free of stubborn Paraguay on Independence Day

children.content: After strolling through the World Cup so far, France faced its first major test against the determined defence of Paraguay. In stifling heat and with tensions boiling over, Les Bleus came through largely unscathed.

image.altText: Rayan, wearing a black hat, has his arm around Didier. Both smile.

items.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

gallery.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

thumbnail.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

items.title: Prince Harry will not be joined by Meghan and children on London visit

children.content: UK media report the decision was taken after a request for police protection was denied.

image.altText: Meghan holds a giggling Lilibet in the air as Harry holds Archie on his knee

items.title: Taylor Swift's wedding a lesson in 'media mastery'

children.content: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

image.altText: A man in a black shirt holds a woman wearing a black and white dress in a warm embrace in a flower-filled garden

items.title: Fran Lebowitz — witty, withering and unfiltered

children.content: No-one emerges unscathed from Fran Lebowitz's quips, bon mots and scathing take-downs of New York's peculiarities and poor etiquette.

gallery.altText: Close up shot of Fran Lebowitz staring down the lens, on stage against a blue curtain wearing a navy jacket and white shirt

thumbnail.altText: Close up shot of Fran Lebowitz staring down the lens, on stage against a blue curtain wearing a navy jacket and white shirt

items.title: When Jackie Chan came to Falls Creek and 'got hypothermia' in a frozen lake

children.content: Thirty years ago a Hollywood crew turned Falls Creek into Ukraine, and Jackie Chan nearly froze getting the shot.

image.altText: Jackie chan in snow outfit and seal beanie smile and stands behind a video camera.

items.title: App fatigue can 'warp' dating but there's an antidote

children.content: Dating apps promised more choice than ever, but experts say the endless swiping and split-second decisions may be leaving users more exhausted, and less connected, than before. But deleting them may not be the only solution.

image.altText: Broken heart emoji on a phone

items.title: 'The world lost him': Crowds gather in Iran to farewell body of assassinated supreme leader

children.content: Millions of Iranians have gathered in Tehran as the country begins a week of funeral ceremonies for former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

gallery.altText: Crowds wearing black and waving flags gather outside a large building, decorated with a large banner of Ali Khamenei.
A crowd of thousands of people wearing black seen from above, the only pops of colour red and green from Iranian flags.
A woman in a black hijab holds up a sign of Trump with a crosshair over his face, and the words "THERE WILL BE BLOOD".

thumbnail.altText: Crowds wearing black and waving flags gather outside a large building, decorated with a large banner of Ali Khamenei.
A crowd of thousands of people wearing black seen from above, the only pops of colour red and green from Iranian flags.
A woman in a black hijab holds up a sign of Trump with a crosshair over his face, and the words "THERE WILL BE BLOOD".

avatar.alt: A woman in a portrait taken against a deep red background.

items.title: The greyhound races Tasmanian authorities don't want you to see

children.content: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

image.altText: A pack of greyhounds racing.

items.title: Australia's spy agency turns to Vogue to find recruits

children.content: A security industry insider tells the ABC the agency cannot afford to "just hire dweebs" as ASIS pivots to a new recruitment strategy aimed at women.

image.altText: Two women in suits sit on a white couch on a stage, with a large Vogue sign in the background.

items.title: Farmers stuck with 'broken' standalone power systems

children.content: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

image.altText: A wide of a man standing in front of a solar panel grid

items.title: NT government at odds with Nhulunbuy economic fund over housing crisis

children.content: A government-funded housing provider in the Northern Territory mining town of Nhulunbuy has been criticised for "not doing enough" to support small business owners.

image.altText: Tradespeople wearing high-vis uniforms working on a construction site in a regional area.

items.title: The spearfisherman who rediscovered a shipwreck

children.content: The startling noise of metal on metal was unusual on a coral reef and unlocked a nearly century-old mystery of a tragic maritime disaster, sunken treasure and a peculiar, inscribed turtle shell.

gallery.altText: Black-and-white image of the sinking of the SS Gothenburg during a storm.

thumbnail.altText: Black-and-white image of the sinking of the SS Gothenburg during a storm.

items.title: The desert kids making classic hard rock cool again

children.content: Inspired by AC/DC and raised in a remote NT community, these young cousins have become one of Australia's most unlikely hard rock bands.

image.altText: Six members of Mulga Bore Hard Rock with black-and-white face paint pose for a group portrait in a studio setting

items.title: Student balloons capture Earth images from 35 kilometres above Cobar

children.content: Students from Newcastle head to outback NSW to launch two balloons into the Earth's upper atmosphere.

gallery.altText: a picture of the earth from space

thumbnail.altText: a picture of the earth from space

items.title: Sydney Trains female apprentices step up amid 94pc gender skew

children.content: Nicola Huggins and Ariana Gackowski are learning the wiring, welding and hand tool skills required to one day repair the trains Sydneysiders travel on every day.

image.altText: Ariana Gackowski (right) and Nicola Huggins in bright orange hazmat suits.

items.title: 'Dismantled from the inside out': Finding connection after child loss

children.content: After Kimberly Stevens lost her teenage son she spent years building an app to help other bereaved parents, and she is now taking its findings to an international audience.

image.altText: A selfie of a woman and young teenage boy smiling at the camera

items.title: Japan releases LGBTQIA+ education plan but some are not happy

children.content: The first national plan of its kind is broadly criticised by gay rights proponents and legal experts for supporting the status quo.

image.altText: Two women give the peace sign in a selfie with a large mountain in the background

items.title: Icy poles and ice baths as Vingegaard claims yellow

children.content: Jonas Vingegaard leads the Tour de France after being delivered to victory in the opening state team time trial by his Visma lease-a-bike squad on the streets of Barcelona.

image.altText: Alpecin Premiertech with icy poles

items.title: Tears flow as 21yo Filipino stuns reigning champion at Wimbledon

children.content: Alexandra Eala is left in tears after her stunning victory over reigning Wimbledon champion Iga Świątek.

articles.title: Knee 'tweak' ends Serena Williams Wimbledon comeback

children.content: Serena Williams has pulled out of her Wimbledon doubles campaign alongside sister Venus, after sustaining a knee injury during her opening-round singles defeat to Australia's Maya Joint.

image.altText: A woman sits on a hospital bed with a woman in a yellow shirt assessing her leg

articles.title: De Minaur defeats 'Aussie slayer' in third round at Wimbledon

children.content: World number 6 Alex de Minaur has won against the USA's Zach Svajda, a victory which will see him progress to the second week of Wimbledon.
